node.name: OpenAI.complete
node.attributes:
{"llm": {"system": "openai", "prompts": ["
    You are a helpful assistant that extracts a scientific question related to medical countermeasures (MCM) from a list of user messages.

    The user messages are provided below. The last entry in the dictionary is the user's latest request:

    <user messages>
    [{'role': 'user', 'content': 'What can I ask?'}]
    </user messages>

    The scientific question that is currently stored in the context is reported below:

    <current question>
    
    </current question>

    Does the user consider a new question in their latest request, without asking for follow-up to the original question? <yes/no>
    Did the user provide any additional information about the original question in their latest request? <yes/no>
    Does the user ask for follow-up to the original question in their latest request? <yes/no>

    If you answered yes to any of these questions, you need to extract a new scientific question of interest from the user messages.         If the user requests non scientific information, such as references or links to datasets, you need to add that to the scientific question.

    If you answered no to all questions, you can return the current question without any changes.

    If the user asks for follow-up to a previous question, you need to consider relevant information from the previous messages.         You need to give more weight to the last messages in the chain when determining the new question. 
    Use the following HTML format to respond. Only report the  question of interest without adding anything else.

    <Answer>
        The detailed description of the question or issue of interest.
    </Answer>

    "], "provider": "openai", "model_name": "gpt-4o", "token_count": {"total": 347, "prompt": 338, "completion": 9, "prompt_details": {"audio": 0, "cache_read": 0}, "completion_details": {"audio": 0, "reasoning": 0}}, "invocation_parameters": "{"context_window":128000,"num_output":-1,"is_chat_model":true,"is_function_calling_model":true,"model_name":"gpt-4o","system_role":"system"}"}, "input": {"value": "{"args": ["\n    You are a helpful assistant that extracts a scientific question related to medical countermeasures (MCM) from a list of user messages.\n\n    The user messages are provided below. The last entry in the dictionary is the user's latest request:\n\n    <user messages>\n    [{'role': 'user', 'content': 'What can I ask?'}]\n    </user messages>\n\n    The scientific question that is currently stored in the context is reported below:\n\n    <current question>\n    \n    </current question>\n\n    Does the user consider a new question in their latest request, without asking for follow-up to the original question? <yes/no>\n    Did the user provide any additional information about the original question in their latest request? <yes/no>\n    Does the user ask for follow-up to the original question in their latest request? <yes/no>\n\n    If you answered yes to any of these questions, you need to extract a new scientific question of interest from the user messages.         If the user requests non scientific information, such as references or links to datasets, you need to add that to the scientific question.\n\n    If you answered no to all questions, you can return the current question without any changes.\n\n    If the user asks for follow-up to a previous question, you need to consider relevant information from the previous messages.         You need to give more weight to the last messages in the chain when determining the new question. \n    Use the following HTML format to respond. Only report the  question of interest without adding anything else.\n\n    <Answer>\n        The detailed description of the question or issue of interest.\n    </Answer>\n\n    "]}", "mime_type": "application/json"}, "output": {"value": "<Answer>
    
    </Answer>"}, "openinference": {"span": {"kind": "LLM"}}}

node.name: OpenAI.complete
node.attributes:
{"llm": {"system": "openai", "prompts": ["
    You are a helpful scientific agent that helps a scientist with medical countermeasure (MCM) research.         You are already connected and have full access to a SQL database with data and information on viruses, bacteria,         their countermeasures and other relevant meta information such as bibliographic references, and links to websites or datasets.

    To help the scientist, you must:
        1. Consider the list of messages between the user and the assistant below to assess:
            1. What has been done already.
            2. What needs to be done next.
        2. Create a list of tasks to perform.

    The list of messages is reported below in JSON format. The latest user message is the last entry in the dictionary.     You need to give more weight to the last messages in the chain when deciding what needs to be done.     Assume that the user is asking questions in the context of these messages, and therefore you may need to pull information from         the user or the assistant

    <user messages>
    [{'role': 'user', 'content': 'What can I ask?'}]
    </user messages>

    The research question has been extracted for you and is reported below:

    <research question>
    
    </research question>

    You need to create a plan for a workflow that will address the user's request. Your plan should be detailed and have         a single task per step.

    Do not decompose the information requested by the user into multiple steps, this will be done later!         ALWAYS keep the entire research question in a single step!!!

    First, check the messages to pull in any clarifying information or other data that was already obtained that the user could be referring to.
    Determine if the user is asking for new data, a reformatting of old information, or both.

    Here is your list of tools. The steps of the plan you generate should map to the tools that you have available to you.
        Do not include more tasks than tools.

    <tools>
    SQLQuery: Useful to search a SQL database for information related to a user query
SQLTest: Useful to test if the SQL database is online
FormatResults: Useful to return a response to the user, with reformatting as needed. It can tell a user about system knowledge. It can also make tables. It does not perform SQL queries.
    </tools>

    If new data is required and the user does not provide specific demands, assume that you need to perform a SQL search as follows:
        1. Check the connection to the SQL database
        2. Search the SQL database for information related to the research question. Search all of the information in a single step!
        3. Return the response to the user, with any reformatting required.

    If new data is required and the user provides specific demands, assume that a SQL search has already been done and that you need to search for more information,         or present the information in a specific format based on the user request.         In that case, create a plan with detailed steps.         If the information requested is already available in the previous user messages, use that and do not run a new query.        In any case, make sure that there is only one task per step.

    If the user is not asking a scientific question that is related to medical countermeasures, viruses, or bacteria, or related to previous results, the only step of the plan should be:
        1. Return a response to the user that the tool is not suited to answering the question.

    Provide the plan in JSON format EXACTLY as follows:

        {
            "task1": description,
            "task2": description,
            ...
        }
    "], "provider": "openai", "model_name": "gpt-4o", "token_count": {"total": 800, "prompt": 770, "completion": 30, "prompt_details": {"audio": 0, "cache_read": 0}, "completion_details": {"audio": 0, "reasoning": 0}}, "invocation_parameters": "{"context_window":128000,"num_output":-1,"is_chat_model":true,"is_function_calling_model":true,"model_name":"gpt-4o","system_role":"system"}"}, "input": {"value": "{"args": ["\n    You are a helpful scientific agent that helps a scientist with medical countermeasure (MCM) research.         You are already connected and have full access to a SQL database with data and information on viruses, bacteria,         their countermeasures and other relevant meta information such as bibliographic references, and links to websites or datasets.\n\n    To help the scientist, you must:\n        1. Consider the list of messages between the user and the assistant below to assess:\n            1. What has been done already.\n            2. What needs to be done next.\n        2. Create a list of tasks to perform.\n\n    The list of messages is reported below in JSON format. The latest user message is the last entry in the dictionary.     You need to give more weight to the last messages in the chain when deciding what needs to be done.     Assume that the user is asking questions in the context of these messages, and therefore you may need to pull information from         the user or the assistant\n\n    <user messages>\n    [{'role': 'user', 'content': 'What can I ask?'}]\n    </user messages>\n\n    The research question has been extracted for you and is reported below:\n\n    <research question>\n    \n    </research question>\n\n    You need to create a plan for a workflow that will address the user's request. Your plan should be detailed and have         a single task per step.\n\n    Do not decompose the information requested by the user into multiple steps, this will be done later!         ALWAYS keep the entire research question in a single step!!!\n\n    First, check the messages to pull in any clarifying information or other data that was already obtained that the user could be referring to.\n    Determine if the user is asking for new data, a reformatting of old information, or both.\n\n    Here is your list of tools. The steps of the plan you generate should map to the tools that you have available to you.\n        Do not include more tasks than tools.\n\n    <tools>\n    SQLQuery: Useful to search a SQL database for information related to a user query\nSQLTest: Useful to test if the SQL database is online\nFormatResults: Useful to return a response to the user, with reformatting as needed. It can tell a user about system knowledge. It can also make tables. It does not perform SQL queries.\n    </tools>\n\n    If new data is required and the user does not provide specific demands, assume that you need to perform a SQL search as follows:\n        1. Check the connection to the SQL database\n        2. Search the SQL database for information related to the research question. Search all of the information in a single step!\n        3. Return the response to the user, with any reformatting required.\n\n    If new data is required and the user provides specific demands, assume that a SQL search has already been done and that you need to search for more information,         or present the information in a specific format based on the user request.         In that case, create a plan with detailed steps.         If the information requested is already available in the previous user messages, use that and do not run a new query.        In any case, make sure that there is only one task per step.\n\n    If the user is not asking a scientific question that is related to medical countermeasures, viruses, or bacteria, or related to previous results, the only step of the plan should be:\n        1. Return a response to the user that the tool is not suited to answering the question.\n\n    Provide the plan in JSON format EXACTLY as follows:\n\n        {\n            \"task1\": description,\n            \"task2\": description,\n            ...\n        }\n    "]}", "mime_type": "application/json"}, "output": {"value": "```json
{
    "task1": "Return a response to the user that the tool is not suited to answering the question."
}
```"}, "openinference": {"span": {"kind": "LLM"}}}

node.name: OpenAI.complete
AgentWorkflow.setup_agent
node.attributes: {"input": {"value": "{\"ctx\": \"<llama_index.core.workflow.context.Context object at 0x7f94140a7b60>\", \"ev\": \"AgentInput(input=[ChatMessage(role=<MessageRole.USER: 'user'>, additional_kwargs={}, blocks=[TextBlock(block_type='text', text='Execute the task')])], current_agent_name='Agent')\"}", "mime_type": "application/json"}, "output": {"value": "{\"input\":[{\"role\":\"system\",\"blocks\":[{\"text\":\"\\n    You are a helpful assistant that selects the best tool for a given task. \\n    \\n    The list of tools and their description are provided below:\\n    \\n    <list of tools>\\n    SQLQuery: Useful to search a SQL database for information related to a user query\\nSQLTest: Useful to test if the SQL database is online\\nFormatResults: Useful to return a response to the user, with reformatting as needed. It can tell a user about system knowledge. It can also make tables. It does not perform SQL queries.\\n    </list of tools>\\n    \\n    The task to accomplish is described below:\\n    \\n    <current task>\\n    Return a response to the user that the tool is not suited to answering the question.\\n    </current task>\\n    \\n    The variables that are available in the context to perform the task are listed below as VARIABLE NAME: DESCRIPTION pairs:\\n    \\n    <context variables>\\n    \\n    </context variables>\\n    \\n    Some information, such as variable names to use for the current task, can also be present in the outcome of the previous tasks.         These outcomes are reported below:\\n    <outcome of previous tasks>\\n    \\n    </outcome of previous tasks>\\n\\n    When you decide on a tool, you need to determine what the inputs to that tool are, then you need to analyse the list of variables provided above    and the outcomes of previous tasks, and identify the variables that need to be passed to the tool.     If you find that you need to pass variables to the tool, you must include the variable names in the tool's input.\\n\\n    In general, you must select the best tool for the task and perform the task.         HOWEVER, there are some exceptions, where you MUST perform the task yourself without calling a tool:\\n    - If the task is simply to identify a tool, just identify the name of the best tool for the task yourself, and do not perform the task. \\n    - If the task is to identify a variable, just identify the name of the variable yourself, and do not perform the task.\\n\\n    For any other task, if there is no tool to perform the task, report that you could not perform your task and that it was a failure.\\n    \\n    When the task is finished, provide a very detailed feedback to the user. Highlight if the task was a success, exceeded expectations, or failed.\\n    \"}]},{\"role\":\"user\",\"blocks\":[{\"text\":\"Execute the task\"}]}],\"current_agent_name\":\"Agent\"}", "mime_type": "application/json"}, "openinference": {"span": {"kind": "CHAIN"}}}

node.name: OpenAI.astream_chat
node.attributes:
{"llm": {"tools": [{"tool": {"json_schema": "{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "SQLQuery", "description": "Useful to search a SQL database for information related to a user query", "parameters": {"properties": {"timeout": {"default": 600, "title": "Timeout", "type": "integer"}, "max_iterations": {"default": 1, "title": "Max Iterations", "type": "integer"}}, "type": "object", "additionalProperties": false}, "strict": false}}"}}, {"tool": {"json_schema": "{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "SQLTest", "description": "Useful to test if the SQL database is online", "parameters": {"properties": {}, "type": "object", "additionalProperties": false}, "strict": false}}"}}, {"tool": {"json_schema": "{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "FormatResults", "description": "Useful to return a response to the user, with reformatting as needed.     You are a helpful assistant that selects the best tool for a given task. 
    
    The list of tools and their description are provided below:
    
    <list of tools>
    SQLQuery: Useful to search a SQL database for information related to a user query
SQLTest: Useful to test if the SQL database is online
FormatResults: Useful to return a response to the user, with reformatting as needed. It can tell a user about system knowledge. It can also make tables. It does not perform SQL queries.
    </list of tools>
    
    The task to accomplish is described below:
    
    <current task>
    Return a response to the user that the tool is not suited to answering the question.
    </current task>
    
    The variables that are available in the context to perform the task are listed below as VARIABLE NAME: DESCRIPTION pairs:
    
    <context variables>
    
    </context variables>
    
    Some information, such as variable names to use for the current task, can also be present in the outcome of the previous tasks.         These outcomes are reported below:
    <outcome of previous tasks>
    
    </outcome of previous tasks>

    When you decide on a tool, you need to determine what the inputs to that tool are, then you need to analyse the list of variables provided above    and the outcomes of previous tasks, and identify the variables that need to be passed to the tool.     If you find that you need to pass variables to the tool, you must include the variable names in the tool's input.

    In general, you must select the best tool for the task and perform the task.         HOWEVER, there are some exceptions, where you MUST perform the task yourself without calling a tool:
    - If the task is simply to identify a tool, just identify the name of the best tool for the task yourself, and do not perform the task. 
    - If the task is to identify a variable, just identify the name of the variable yourself, and do not perform the task.

    For any other task, if there is no tool to perform the task, report that you could not perform your task and that it was a failure.
    
    When the task is finished, provide a very detailed feedback to the user. Highlight if the task was a success, exceeded expectations, or failed.
    "}}, {"message": {"role": "user", "content": "Execute the task"}}], "output_messages": [{"message": {"role": "assistant", "content": "To accomplish the task of returning a response to the user that the tool is not suited to answering the question, I will use the `FormatResults` tool. These outcomes are reported below:\\n    <outcome of previous tasks>\\n    \\n    </outcome of previous tasks>\\n\\n    When you decide on a tool, you need to determine what the inputs to that tool are, then you need to analyse the list of variables provided above    and the outcomes of previous tasks, and identify the variables that need to be passed to the tool. Highlight if the task was a success, exceeded expectations, or failed.\\n    \")])", "ChatMessage(role=<MessageRole.USER: 'user'>, additional_kwargs={}, blocks=[TextBlock(block_type='text', text='Execute the task')])"], "kwargs": {"tools": [{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "SQLQuery", "description": "Useful to search a SQL database for information related to a user query", "parameters": {"properties": {"timeout": {"default": 600, "title": "Timeout", "type": "integer"}, "max_iterations": {"default": 1, "title": "Max Iterations", "type": "integer"}}, "type": "object", "additionalProperties": false}, "strict": false}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "SQLTest", "description": "Useful to test if the SQL database is online", "parameters": {"properties": {}, "type": "object", "additionalProperties": false}, "strict": false}}, {"type": "function", "function": {"name": "FormatResults", "description": "Useful to return a response to the user, with reformatting as needed. node.attributes: {"input": {"value": "{\"ctx\": \"<llama_index.core.workflow.context.Context object at 0x7f94140a7b60>\", \"ev\": \"AgentSetup(input=[ChatMessage(role=<MessageRole.SYSTEM: 'system'>, additional_kwargs={}, blocks=[TextBlock(block_type='text', text=\\\"\\\\n    You are a helpful assistant that selects the best tool for a given task. \\\\n    \\\\n    The list of tools and their description are provided below:\\\\n    \\\\n    <list of tools>\\\\n    SQLQuery: Useful to search a SQL database for information related to a user query\\\\nSQLTest: Useful to test if the SQL database is online\\\\nFormatResults: Useful to return a response to the user, with reformatting as needed.
